CINDY
Maybe he got out through the
window?
CARLA —
No, the windows were locked.
CINDY
I don't understand. You were --
attacked, or you weren't -- ?
CARLA

Of course I was. He beat me.

He almost strangled me. Then
he -- raped me. And when it was
over, he vanished -- like he was
never there --

INT. GIRLS' BEDROOM ~- NIGHT

Carla and the children are seated on the bed, playing

ecards. Carla, Billy and

Julie seem distracted. Only

Kim is really playing the game. She gazes at her

mother petulantly.

KIM
Mommy, it's your turn.

The front DOORBELL RINGS.

INT. MORAN LIVING ROOM -

NIGHT

Billy precedes Carla through the hallway and living

room to the front door.

INT./EXT. THROUGH WINDSHIELD - PIER APPROACH (SLOW MOTION)
- DAY

The car careens over the sidewalk. Two men, lounging in
the shadows of the building, seem to fly back as the car
lurches toward them. In an abstraction, the window breaks
in like a wave as the car hits a pole.

EXT. BUICK (SLOW MOTION)

As it impacts against the pole, the metal grille buckles,
fenders and interior engine parts are thrown from the
ripped hood. Then everything is still.

SLOW EFFECT DISSOLVE TO:
INT. NEUROPSYCHIATRIC CLINIC - WEST COAST UNIVERSITY ~. DAY

UNFOCUSSED SHOT of Carla, a deformed reflection seen in the
glassed-in partition of the examining room: a small cubicle
devoid of decor and spartanly furnished. Her face is bruised
and bandaged. She is sitting on a chair, folding and unfolding
her hands, as CAMERA FOCUSSES. She is watching someone o.s.,
like a scared animal, seems on the edge of losing control.
CAMERA PANS to GARY SNEIDERMAN, sitting across from her.

GARY -_
I'm impressed.

CARLA
By what?

GARY

Your independence. Knowing who
you are and what you want.
Keeping your family together.
And doing it the hard way.

Carla lowers her eyes demurely, then smiles, genuinely
touched.

CARLA
I'm glad you approve of me.
“THE ENTITY" ~ Rev. 3/25/81 49.

- DR. WALCOTT
But you cannot ~~ why?

CARLA
Because he hurt Billy. And
because of the marks on my body.
They're in places where I can't
do it to myself. Even in a dream.

DR. WILKES
Why does he attack you, Carla, and
not someone else?

CARLA
I suppose he chose me.

DR. WILKES
Chose you? Why?

CARLA
I don't know. I suppose he finds
me attractive.

Carla is blushing.

I'm fine. ¢ MORE)

79,

CARLA (CONT'D)
Are the children there?
(her eyes cloud)
Yes, all right, put him on --

She bites her lip. For a moment, she holds the receiver
away from her. Then she brings it closer again.

CARLA
(in a strange
voice)
Hello, Billy ~- how are you?

Are the girls all right --?

Behind her, the window curtain billows inward.

You said this candlestick just
flew off the mant lepiece and hit
your son?

: CARLA
Yes. It almost broke his arm.

KRAFT
Where are your children now,
Mrs. Moran? .

CARLA :
With my friend, Cindy. They stay
there whenever possible. It's
dangerous for them here -- especially
at night.

CAMERA MOVES IN ON CARLA

Suddenly, her nostrils flare, as an odor assails her. She
speaks in a whisper.

CARLA
Do you smell it?

KRAFT
She saw him, Dr. Cooley!

DR. COOLEY
But you didn't. Nor did Joe.
Nor did your cameras. The finest
lenses, the fastest film couldn't
even catch a wisp of him. Isn't
it possible she's hallucinating?

Kraft has just finished developing another photograph.
He whisks it out of the hypo and with a flourish:

273° CONTINUED:

SP KRAFT
One of them caught this.
Dr. Cooley studies the photograph, seems impressed, and
yet:
DR. COOLEY

Kraft looks uncertainly at Gary.

GARY
I'm a resident in the school of
psychiatry.

A wave of hostility emanates from Kraft. He immediately
clams up and stalks off with his equipment.

CARLA
Would you like some coffee, Doctor?
He turns to face her -- fighting his anger.
GARY
Thank you.

EXT. MORAN BACKYARD ~ DAY

Carla and Gary are on the back porch, sipping coffee.
Carla sits on the wooden railing, not looking at him.

GARY
Why aren't you coming back, Carla?

KRAFT
Somebody, turn on the lights!
Open the window and get some
air in here.

WIDE ANGLE

The windows and doors are opened, and when the lights are
switched on, we SEE the blank, ashen faces of the witnesses.
For a long time, no one speaks. Dr. Cooley seems lost in
the depths of her own thoughts, as she watches Carla, being
ministered by several girls, who bring her water. Then,

Dr. Cooley turns and slowly leaves the room, with Kraft,
Mehan and a group of other students.

INT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE BEDROOM

Emerging from the bedroom, Dr. Cooley continues to muse
over the startling phenomenon she has just witnessed.

KRAFT
What do you think?

GARY
Somebody has to look after her.

Dr. Weber studies Gary closely. For the first time, he
sees how deeply involved with Carla he is.

DR. WEBER
(gently)
Let her go, Gary! You have no
choice. Professionally -- and

personally.

Gary looks at Dr. Weber, presenting a face that expresses
the extent of his emotional dilemma.

GARY
I can't let her go!
_ DR. WEBER
(sternly)
‘ed What do you mean, you can't!?

GARY

415 THE HOSES

The steam gains in force as Carla continues shouting

below.
CARLA (0.S.)
It took me going through hell
and back. But I came up with
it.
416 GARY

runs around the shambles of the simulated house, stopping
short at an opening vent out of the wall through which
Carla can be seen, shouting up at the steaming assembly
with a crazed voice. ,

CARLA
I can stand up to all of it.
While the rest of you won't show
your faces. Not you! Bastard!

CLOSER ANGLE

Carla steps out of the taxi weakly. Her dress is tattered,

-her hair is in wild disarray; her expression is lost and

tortured. She asks the driver to wait, then turns to the
house. She stares at it starkly. Then, taking a deep
breath, she walks purposefully up the walk and climbs the
porch.

INT. MORAN LIVING ROOM - DAY

Though the shades are drawn, we can HEAR Carla's FOOTSTEPS
and perceive her shadow as she mounts the porch steps and
walks up to the door. Without hesitation, the knob turns
and the door opens. The light from without frames Carla
in silhouette and reveals a room barren of furniture and
furnishings. Fearlessly, Carla walks into the center of
the living room, leaving the front door open behind her.
She glances at the hare walls, rugless floor and peeling
ceiling, then walks into:

THE HALLWAY ~ DAY
